title: New payment rails aim to make small trade truly borderless
dek: A coalition of fintech firms is testing faster settlement for the merchants who move Africa’s everyday economy.
author: Tobi Adeyemi
body: For traders, the promise of a continental market is often slowed by the practical problem of getting paid. A new pilot links mobile wallets, banks and merchant accounts across several currencies.
Regulators are working on shared consumer protections, while operators are trying to keep fees low enough for small transactions.
If the pilot scales, its biggest impact may be felt in the kiosks, markets and logistics depots where cross-border commerce already happens.
